community.cassandra.cassandra_assassinat.short_description: Run the assassinate command against a node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_assassinat.description: Run the assassinate command against a node. Forcefully removes a dead node without re-replicating any data. It is a last resort tool if you cannot successfully use nodetool removenode.

ip_address.description: IP Address of endpoint to assassinate.

keyspace.description: The keyspace on which to change autocompact status.

table.description: The table on which to change autocompact status.

community.cassandra.cassandra_batchlogre.short_description: Sets the batch log replay throttle.
community.cassandra.cassandra_batchlogre.description: Sets the batch log replay throttle.

value.description: KB value to set batch log replay throttle to.

community.cassandra.cassandra_binary.short_description: Enables or disables the binary protocol.
community.cassandra.cassandra_binary.description: Enables or disables the binary protocol, also known as the native transport.

community.cassandra.cassandra_cleanup.short_description: Runs cleanup on a Cassandra node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_cleanup.description: Runs cleanup on a Cassandra node.

table.description: Optional table name or list of table names.

community.cassandra.cassandra_compact.short_description: Manage compaction on the Cassandra node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_compact.description: Manage compaction on the Cassandra node.

keyspace.description: The keyspace to adjust compaction thresholds for.

table.description: The table to adjust compaction thresholds for.

min.description: Sets the minimum number of SSTables to trigger a minor compaction when using SizeTieredCompactionStrategy or DateTieredCompactionStrategy.

max.description: Sets the maximum number of SSTables to allow in a minor compaction when using SizeTieredCompactionStrategy or DateTieredCompactionStrategy.

value.description: MB value to set compaction throughput to.

community.cassandra.cassandra_concurrenc.short_description: Manage concurrency parameters on the Cassandra node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_concurrenc.description: Manage concurrency parameters on the Cassandra node. Set maximum concurrency for processing stage. Set number of concurrent compactors. Set the number of concurrent view builders in the system.

concurrency_stage.description: The processing stage Only relevent when concurrency_type is default

community.cassandra.cassandra_cqlsh.short_description: Run cql commands via the clqsh shell.
community.cassandra.cassandra_cqlsh.description: Run cql commands via the clqsh shell. Run commands inline or using a cql file. Attempts to parse returned data into a format that Ansible can use.

username.description: The C* user to authenticate with.

file.description: Path to a file containing cql commands.

encoding.description: Specify a non-default encoding for output.

cqlshrc.description: Specify an alternative cqlshrc file location.

connect_timeout.description: Specify the connection timeout in seconds.

request_timeout.description: Specify the default request timeout in seconds.

transform.description: Transform the output returned to the user. auto - Attempt to automatically decide the best tranformation. split - Split output on a character. json - parse as json. raw - Return the raw output.

split_char.description: Used by the split action in the transform stage.

additional_args.description: Additional arguments to supply to the mongo command. Supply as key-value pairs. If the parameter is a valueless flag supply a bool value.

community.cassandra.cassandra_decommissi.short_description: Deactivates a node by streaming its data to another node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_decommissi.description: Deactivates a node by streaming its data to another node. Uses the nodetool ring command to determine if the node is still in the cluster. To ensure correct function of this module please use the ip address of the node in the host parameter.

debug.description: Add additional debug to module output.

community.cassandra.cassandra_drain.description: Flushes all memtables from the node to SSTables on disk. Cassandra stops listening for connections from the client and other nodes. Restart Cassandra after running nodetool drain. Use this command before upgrading a node to a new version of Cassandra.

community.cassandra.cassandra_flush.short_description: Flushes one or more tables from the memtable to SSTables on disk.
community.cassandra.cassandra_flush.description: Flushes one or more tables from the memtable to SSTables on disk.

table.description: Optional table name or list of table names.

community.cassandra.cassandra_fullqueryl.short_description: Manages the full query log feature.
community.cassandra.cassandra_fullqueryl.description: Manages the full query log feature. Enable, disable or reset feature. Manage configuration. Supported from Cassandra 4.0 onwards. When state is disabled the value of the other configuration options are ignored. The module always returns changed when state is reset.

archive_command.description: Command that will handle archiving rolled full query log files. Format is "/path/to/script.sh %path" where %path will be replaced with the file to archive.

roll_cycle.description: How often to roll the log file.

blocking.description: If the queue is full whether to block producers or drop samples.

max_log_size.description: How many bytes of log data to store before dropping segments.

max_queue_weight.description: Maximum number of bytes of query data to queue to disk before blocking or dropping samples.

community.cassandra.cassandra_garbagecol.short_description: Removes deleted data from one or more tables.
community.cassandra.cassandra_garbagecol.description: Removes deleted data from one or more tables.

keyspace.description: Keyspace to clean up data from.

table.description: Table to clean up deleted data from.

granularity.description: ROW (default) removes deleted partitions and rows. CELL also removes overwritten or deleted cells.

jobs.description: Number of SSTables affected simultaneously. Set to 0 to use all compaction threads.

community.cassandra.cassandra_handoff.short_description: Enables or disables the storing of future hints on the current node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_handoff.description: Enables or disables the storing of future hints on the current node.

value.description: MB value to set inter-dc stream throughput to.

community.cassandra.cassandra_invalidate.short_description: Invalidates the various caches on the Cassandra node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_invalidate.description: Invalidates the various caches on the Cassandra node. Invalidates the Counter, Key and Row caches.

fake_counter.description: Fake the counter results so the module wil execute the appropriate invalid cache command. Intended for internal testing use only.

community.cassandra.cassandra_keyspace.short_description: Manage keyspaces on your Cassandra cluster.
community.cassandra.cassandra_keyspace.description: Manage keyspaces on your Cassandra Cluster. Keyspace can be created to use SimpleStrategy or NetworkTopologyStrategy. Keyspace modifications are supported, for example durable writes, replication factor or data centre changes but it is not supported to migrate between replication strategies i.e. NetworkTopologyStrategy -> SimpleStrategy.

login_user.description: The Cassandra user to login with.

login_password.description: The Cassandra password to login with.

ssl.description: Uses SSL encryption if basic SSL encryption is enabled on Cassandra cluster (without client/server verification)

ssl_ca_certs.description: The SSL CA chain or certificate location to confirm supplied certificate validity (required when ssl_cert_reqs is set to CERT_OPTIONAL or CERT_REQUIRED)

login_host.description: The Cassandra hostname. If unset the instance will check 127.0.0.1 for a C* instance. Otherwise the value returned by socket.getfqdn() is used.

name.description: The name of the keyspace to create or manage.

state.description: The desired state of the keyspace.

replication_factor.description: The total number of copies of your keyspace data. The keyspace is created with SimpleStrategy. If data_centres is set this parameter is ignored. If not supplied the default value will be used.

durable_writes.description: Enable durable writes for the keyspace. If not supplied the default value will be used.

data_centres.description: The keyspace will be created with NetworkTopologyStrategy. Specify your data centres, along with replication_factor, as key-value pairs.

consistency_level.description: Consistency level to perform cassandra queries with. Not all consistency levels are supported by read or write connections.\ When a level is not supported then LOCAL_ONE, the default is used. Consult the README.md on GitHub for further details.

community.cassandra.cassandra_maxhintwin.short_description: Set the specified max hint window in ms.
community.cassandra.cassandra_maxhintwin.description: Set the specified max hint window in ms.

value.description: MS value to set the max hint window to.

community.cassandra.cassandra_reload.short_description: Reloads various objects into the local node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_reload.description: Reloads various objects into the local node. Currently can reload local schema, seeds, ssl certs and triggers.

community.cassandra.cassandra_removenode.short_description: Removes a node by the given host id from the cluster.
community.cassandra.cassandra_removenode.description: Removes a node by the given host id from the cluster. Identify the node by the host id as given in nodetool status output. The nodetool status command is used to determine if the host_id exists in the cluster.

host_id.description: Host Id of the node to rmeove.

force.description: Forces completion of the pending removal.

debug.description: Add additional debug to module output.

community.cassandra.cassandra_role.short_description: Manage roles on your Cassandra cluster.
community.cassandra.cassandra_role.description: Manage roles on your Cassandra Cluster.

login_user.description: The Cassandra user to login with.

login_password.description: The Cassandra password to login with.

ssl.description: Uses SSL encryption if basic SSL encryption is enabled on Cassandra cluster (without client/server verification)

ssl_ca_certs.description: The SSL CA chain or certificate location to confirm supplied certificate validity (required when  ssl_cert_reqs is set to CERT_OPTIONAL or CERT_REQUIRED)

name.description: The name of the role to create or manage.

state.description: The desired state of the role.

super_user.description: If the user is a super user or not.

login.description: True allows the role to log in. Use true to create login accounts for internal authentication, PasswordAuthenticator or DSE Unified Authenticator.

update_password.description: Passwords are not handled by default. With this set to true, passwords are always overridden. The task will always be considered changed if this is set to true.

options.description: Reserved for use with authentication plug-ins. Refer to the authenticator documentation for details.

data_centres.description: Only relevant if a network_authorizer has been configured. Specify data centres as keys of this dict. Can specify a key as 'all' although this implicity assumed by Cassandra if not supplied.

keyspace_permissions.description: Grant privileges on keyspace objects. Specify keyspaces as keys of this dict. Permissions supplied as a list to the keyspace keys. Valid permissions at keyspace level are as follows; ALL PERMISSIONS, CREATE, ALTER, AUTHORIZE, DROP, MODIFY, SELECT A special key 'all_keyspaces' can be supplied to assign permissions to all keyspaces.

roles.description: One or more roles to grant to this user or role. When set to None, the default, no action is perform on roles. Set to an empty list to revoke all roles.

consistency_level.description: Consistency level to perform cassandra queries with. Not all consistency levels are supported by read or write connections.\ When a level is not supported then LOCAL_ONE, the default is used. Consult the README.md on GitHub for further details.

community.cassandra.cassandra_schema.short_description: Validates the schema version as seen from the node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_schema.description: Validates the schema version as seen from the node. Ensure that all nodes are have the same schema version. Can poll multiple times to wait for the schema version to converge. Can also specify a schema version if required. Schema version is obtained through the usage of the nodetool describecluster command.

poll.description: The maximum number of times to call nodetool describecluster.

interval.description: The number of seconds to wait between poll executions.

community.cassandra.cassandra_status.short_description: Validates the status of the cluster as seen from the node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_status.description: Validates the status of the cluster as seen from the C* node. Ensure that all nodes are in a UP/NORMAL state or tolerate a few down nodes. Optionally poll multiple times to allow the cluster state to stablise. Cluster status is obtained thtough the usage of the nodetool status command.

down.description: The maximum number of nodes that can be tolerated as down.

poll.description: The maximum number of times to call nodetool status to query cluster status.

interval.description: The number of seconds to wait between poll executions.

value.description: MB value to set stream throughput to.

community.cassandra.cassandra_table.short_description: Create or drop tables on a Cassandra Keyspace.
community.cassandra.cassandra_table.description: Create or drop tables on a Cassandra Keyspace. No alter functionality. If a table with the same name already exists then no changes are made.

login_user.description: The Cassandra user to login with.

login_password.description: The Cassandra password to login with.

ssl.description: Uses SSL encryption if basic SSL encryption is enabled on Cassandra cluster (without client/server verification)

ssl_ca_certs.description: The SSL CA chain or certificate location to confirm supplied certificate validity (required when ssl_cert_reqs is set to CERT_OPTIONAL or CERT_REQUIRED)

name.description: The name of the table to create or drop.

state.description: The desired state of the table.

keyspace.description: The keyspace in which to create the table.

columns.description: The columns for the table. Specifiy pairs as <column name>: <data type>

primary_key.description: The Primary key speicfication for the table

is_type.description: Create a type instead of a table

consistency_level.description: Consistency level to perform cassandra queries with. Not all consistency levels are supported by read or write connections.\ When a level is not supported then LOCAL_ONE, the default is used. Consult the README.md on GitHub for further details.

community.cassandra.cassandra_thrift.short_description: Enables or disables the Thrift server.
community.cassandra.cassandra_thrift.description: Enables or disables the Thrift server.

community.cassandra.cassandra_timeout.short_description: Manages the timeout on the Cassandra node.
community.cassandra.cassandra_timeout.description: Manages the timeout. Set the specified timeout in ms, or 0 to disable timeout.

community.cassandra.cassandra_truncatehi.short_description: Truncate all hints on the local node, or truncate hints for the endpoint(s) specified.
community.cassandra.cassandra_truncatehi.description: Truncate all hints on the local node, or truncate hints for the endpoint(s) specified.

community.cassandra.cassandra_upgradesst.short_description: Upgrade SSTables which are not on the current Cassandra version.
community.cassandra.cassandra_upgradesst.description: Upgrade SSTables which are not on the current Cassandra version. Use this module when upgrading your server or changing compression options.

table.description: Optional table name or list of table names.

community.cassandra.cassandra_verify.short_description: Checks the data checksum for one or more tables.
community.cassandra.cassandra_verify.description: Checks the data checksum for one or more tables.

table.description: Optional table name or list of table names.

extended.description: Extended verify. Each cell data, beyond simply checking SSTable checksums.
